svg.text: "If these three things were not found in the world,
the Tathāgata would not arise, nor would the Dhamma shine in the world."
Birth, Aging, and Death (jāti · jarā · maraṇa)
But because these three exist, the Tathāgata arises and the Dhamma shines in the world.
BIRTH, AGING, AND DEATH can be given up
When passion, aversion, and delusion are given up
THREE ROOTS · TAYO AKUSALAMŪLĀ can be given up
THREE FETTERS · TAYO SAṀYOJANĀ can be given up
UNWISE ATTENTION AND MISDIRECTION can be given up
FORGETFULNESS AND SCATTERED AWARENESS can be given up
DISREGARD FOR THE NOBLE ONES can be given up
MISCONDUCT AND NON-RESTRAINT can be given up
LACK OF COMMITMENT AND EFFORT can be given up
DISRESPECT AND BAD ASSOCIATION can be given up
Sense of Right and Wrong (hirīmā) → Concern (ottāpī) → Diligence (appamatto) →
Having Good Friends (kalyāṇamitto) → Being Energetic (āraddhavīriyo) → Virtuous Conduct (sīlavā) →
Without a fault-finding attitude (anupārambhacitto) → Settled Mind (avikkhittacitto) →
→ Freed from Passion (rāga) · Aversion (dosa) · Delusion (moha) → Capable of giving up birth, aging, and death
